task_completion.description: Read the scheduling request file to get appointment details
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal window displaying the contents of /home/ga/Desktop/scheduling_request.txt, showing patient, provider, date, and time details.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the NOSH ChartingSystem login page
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser open to http://localhost displaying the NOSH ChartingSystem login screen.

task_completion.description: Log into the NOSH ChartingSystem using provided credentials
task_completion.visual_evidence: The NOSH ChartingSystem dashboard or main interface is visible, indicating a successful login as the admin user.

task_completion.description: Open the scheduling or calendar interface
task_completion.visual_evidence: The calendar, schedule, or appointment management screen is actively displayed in the NOSH application.

task_completion.description: Enter the correct appointment details into the scheduling form
task_completion.visual_evidence: An appointment creation modal or form is visible with 'Office Visit' selected as the visit type, 'Dr. James Carter' selected as the provider, and the patient name filled in.

task_completion.description: Confirm the appointment is successfully scheduled on the calendar
task_completion.visual_evidence: The calendar view shows a new appointment block for the patient with Dr. James Carter at the requested date and time, or a clear success message confirming the appointment creation is visible.

integrity.description: The agent used a web browser GUI to interact with the NOSH system
integrity.visual_evidence: NOSH ChartingSystem is accessed via a standard web browser (e.g., Firefox, Chromium) rather than through terminal API/curl commands.

integrity.description: The agent actually read the scheduling request file
integrity.visual_evidence: The file /home/ga/Desktop/scheduling_request.txt is visibly opened in an editor (like gedit, nano) or printed to the terminal (e.g., using cat).

integrity.description: The agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the appointment
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser's developer tools (Inspect Element) are not open to manipulate the DOM to fake an appointment block or success message.

privileged_info_for_vlm: NOSH ChartingSystem is accessed via http://localhost. Admin credentials are 'admin' / 'Admin1234!'. The provider to be scheduled is 'Dr. James Carter' and the visit type must be 'Office Visit'. The patient, date, and time details must match the contents of /home/ga/Desktop/scheduling_request.txt.
